the_signal.headline: Agent skill self-improvement moves from concept to reviewable workflows
the_signal.body: n8n's cognee integration shows agents catching their own failures and proposing fixes with diffs you approve before auto-deployment. This is the production pattern for reducing hallucination feedback loops — your agents now self-correct instead of requiring manual retraining cycles. For GTM teams running lead qualification or discovery agents, this cuts iteration time from days to hours.

tool_drop.name: Senior SWE-Bench
tool_drop.what: Open-source benchmark that evaluates AI agents on senior engineer-level coding tasks, not junior-level toy problems.
tool_drop.why: If you're building code-generation revenue systems or internal tool agents, you need honest benchmarks that test agent reasoning at production complexity — this replaces the inflated evals that vendors ship.

thread_of_day.title: CursorBench 3.1: Agent coding capability gap widens
thread_of_day.takeaway: Latest coding agent benchmarks expose the delta between top performers and mid-tier models — routing strategy (which model for which task) is now table stakes for GTM systems using code generation.

quote.text: When a review run falls short, this workflow catches it and proposes a fix to the skill, then writes the improved instructions back once you approve the diff.
quote.source: n8n Blog — Build Self-Improving Agent Skills

whats_moving.context: n8n's comparison framework (scale ceiling, metadata filtering, overhead) signals RAG pipelines are moving past proof-of-concept into architectural trade-off decisions for production GTM stacks.
Auth, tool-call scoping, and observability are now the gatekeepers for enterprise agent deployments — security theater no longer flies with revenue-critical workflows.

whats_moving.topic: AI maturity frameworks becoming governance standard
whats_moving.context: n8n's 5-level framework (Usage, Skills, Governance, Integration, Optimization) gives GTM leaders language to budget and prioritize agent rollout instead of treating it as feature work.

act_on_this: Map your agent workflows against n8n's self-improvement pattern: identify which steps fail silently today and design a review loop that lets agents propose fixes before you approve them into production.

numbers.stat: Senior SWE-Bench tests agents on real codebase problems vs. isolated coding tasks
numbers.context: Most AI benchmarks reward narrow task completion; this one tests reasoning across context and complexity — closer to how agents will actually perform in your GTM stack.
